title: Synthetic anode strengthens as natural remains rangebound:
gist: Synthetic graphite anode active material (AAM) prices rose in Q2 2026 on strengthening demand and higher feedstock costs, while natural graphite AAM stayed rangebound amid weaker demand and substitution pressure, with low-energy synthetic AAM crossing above $2,900/tonne to exceed natural graphite pricing for the first time since May 2023.

insights.claim: In Q2 2026, synthetic graphite anode active material (AAM) prices rose as demand strengthened and feedstock costs increased, while natural graphite AAM prices remained broadly unchanged amid weaker relative demand, substitution pressure and stable feedstock prices.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: A clear chemistry-level price split emerging within the anode market means blended graphite price benchmarks are becoming less useful; investors need grade-specific exposure views.
insights.quote: Synthetic graphite AAM prices increased as demand strengthened and feedstock costs rose. Natural graphite AAM prices, meanwhile, remained broadly unchanged amidst weaker relative demand, substitution pressure and relatively stable feedstock prices.

insights.claim: The price divergence between synthetic and natural graphite AAM became most pronounced in June 2026, when synthetic graphite producers began passing through earlier feedstock cost increases to buyers.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Feedstock cost pass-through returning to synthetic graphite producers signals improved pricing power and margin recovery potential for that segment after a period of pressure.
insights.quote: The divergence was most evident in June, when synthetic graphite producers began passing through earlier feedstock cost increases.

insights.claim: Low-energy synthetic graphite AAM prices rose above $2,900/tonne in Q2 2026, reaching parity with and then exceeding natural graphite AAM prices for the first time since May 2023.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: The cost crossover erases synthetic graphite's traditional price premium-inversion versus natural graphite, which could reaccelerate cell-maker substitution toward natural/coated spherical graphite for margin-sensitive applications and is a concrete price level to track for the next reversal.
insights.quote: low-energy synthetic AAM prices rose above $2,900/tonne, reaching parity with and then exceeding natural graphite AAM for the first time since May 2023.

insights.claim: Natural graphite AAM faced substitution pressure in Q2 2026, which alongside stable feedstock costs contributed to its rangebound pricing for the quarter.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Substitution pressure on natural graphite is worth monitoring as a demand-share risk for natural graphite miners/processors even as its absolute pricing stays stable.
